rulings.Case Title: DORIS MORALES et al VS. FRANCES ALVARADO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 26, 2010, line 6. DEFENDANTS DAVID ALVARADO, FRANCISCA ALVARADO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled on the grounds that the 3-Day Notice is defective under 15 U.S.C.S. 1692(a)(3)-(4). This presents a factual issue and no showing that plaintiff's counsel is a "debt collector" such that the FDCPA should apply. The demurrer is sustained with 5 days leave to amend on the grounds of uncertainty under CCP section 430(g). Plaintiffs must unambiguously plead whether the agreement waws written or oral. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CLARK L. FONG et al VS. SHARIE M. CANJA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 26, 2010, line 16. DEFENDANTS SHARIE CANJA AND YVETTE TORRES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 5 days leave to amend. The prayer should state the past due rent of $3825 demanded in the notice and damages on a daily basis for possession after the end of June. (See Rutter Cal. Practice Guide Landlord-Tenant Sections 8:59-8:60.)The court would overrule the demurrer on the argument that the 2 notices create uncertainty. the notices are clear and unambiguous. =(302/CWW)
